normative_definition: A curated collection of metadata about resources such as datasets and data services.
intended_capability: Represent a machine-readable catalogue containing dataset and service descriptions.
known_limit: The class does not prescribe sector-specific catalogue obligations cardinalities or metadata truth.

normative_definition: A record describing the registration of one resource in a catalog.
intended_capability: Separate metadata-record provenance and modification from the described resource.
known_limit: Does not itself identify the complete legal or editorial history of the described resource.

normative_definition: A collection of data published or curated by one agent and available for access or download in one or more representations.
intended_capability: Identify the conceptual dataset governed by catalogue and access requirements.
known_limit: The class directly represents dataset identity. By itself, it does not establish the dataset's instrument-specific legal classification, protected ground, relevant version boundary, third-party rights, or the facts determining legal applicability. DCAT does not prohibit adding those descriptions, but they require other properties, profiles, evidence, or legal determinations.

normative_definition: A specific representation of a dataset that may differ by serialization delivery or other manifestation characteristics.
intended_capability: Identify an accessible or downloadable representation separately from the conceptual dataset.
known_limit: A distribution record does not prove availability integrity or authorization.

normative_definition: A collection of operations that provides access to one or more datasets or data-processing functions.
intended_capability: Represent an API or other operational access service.
known_limit: Does not natively describe authentication rate limits availability latency or other service-quality commitments.

normative_definition: Relates a catalog to a dataset that forms part of it.
intended_capability: Publish datasets as members of a catalogue.
known_limit: No minimum catalogue membership cardinality is imposed by DCAT 3.

normative_definition: Relates a dataset to one of its available distributions.
intended_capability: Link a conceptual dataset to access or download manifestations.
known_limit: Does not require that a distribution exist or remain available.

normative_definition: A URL that gives access to a distribution; it may resolve to information on how access is obtained rather than directly to the data.
intended_capability: Represent where a distribution can be accessed.
known_limit: Resolution success authorization and machine-actionable access conditions remain external.

normative_definition: A URL that is a direct link to a downloadable file in a given format.
intended_capability: Represent a direct machine-download location.
known_limit: Does not prove file persistence checksum validity access authority or licence.

normative_definition: Relates a distribution to a data service through which it can be accessed.
intended_capability: Connect a distribution to its API or other access service.
known_limit: Does not describe the access protocol or service-level conditions by itself.

normative_definition: The root location or primary endpoint of a service expressed as an IRI.
intended_capability: Identify the technical service endpoint used for automated access.
known_limit: Endpoint reachability authentication and quality of service are not established.

normative_definition: A description of services available through endpoints including operations and parameters.
intended_capability: Link machine- or human-readable interface documentation.
known_limit: DCAT does not prescribe an API-description language or completeness test.

normative_definition: Relates a data service to a dataset that it can distribute.
intended_capability: Identify which dataset an access service exposes.
known_limit: Does not prove that all versions or distributions of the dataset are served.

normative_definition: Information about who may access a resource or an indication of its security status.
intended_capability: State high-level public restricted or non-public access conditions.
known_limit: A supplied statement does not prove the legal basis correctness or enforceability of access restrictions.

normative_definition: A name given to the dataset; repeatable for parallel language versions.
intended_capability: Provide the mandatory human-readable dataset name.
known_limit: A title alone does not describe source scope characteristics nature or conditions of availability.

normative_definition: A free-text account of the dataset; repeatable for parallel language versions.
intended_capability: Provide a mandatory general dataset account.
known_limit: The property can contain legally relevant information in free text, but by itself does not make each required information item separately identifiable or testable and does not distinguish omission from non-applicability. This is a limitation of the selected mapping function, not a prohibition on the property's content.

normative_definition: Information indicating whether a dataset is public restricted or non-public.
intended_capability: Classify dataset accessibility using the EU access-right authority table.
known_limit: Optional in base DCAT-AP and too coarse to express detailed conditions or authorization.

normative_definition: Legislation mandating creation or management of the dataset.
intended_capability: Link a dataset description to legislation relevant to its creation or management.
known_limit: The property scope is narrower than every law that may apply and assertion does not prove legal applicability or compliance.

normative_definition: A language of the dataset; repeatable where the dataset has multiple languages.
intended_capability: Identify languages of dataset content.
known_limit: Does not by itself record the language coverage of every metadata field or satisfy translation obligations.

normative_definition: A name given to the data service; repeatable for parallel language versions.
intended_capability: Provide the mandatory service name.
known_limit: Does not describe operations access conditions or service quality.

normative_definition: The root location or primary endpoint of the service expressed as an IRI.
intended_capability: Require at least one service endpoint for automated access metadata.
known_limit: Does not test reachability authentication rate limits latency availability or transmitted formats.

normative_definition: A description of endpoint operations parameters and other service-instance details.
intended_capability: Link technical API or endpoint documentation.
known_limit: Optional and not constrained to a machine-processable interface-description format.

normative_definition: A dataset that the data service can distribute.
intended_capability: Connect access services to the datasets they expose.
known_limit: Optional and does not verify actual service coverage.

normative_definition: A URL giving access to a dataset distribution or information on obtaining it.
intended_capability: Require an access location for every described distribution.
known_limit: Existence of an IRI does not prove availability authorization or machine actionability.

normative_definition: Legislation mandating creation or management of the distribution.
intended_capability: Link a distribution to governing creation or management legislation.
known_limit: Assertion is descriptive and does not establish applicability legality or compliance.

normative_definition: A policy expressing rights associated with the distribution when ODRL is used.
intended_capability: Attach a machine-readable permissions prohibitions and duties policy to a distribution.
known_limit: The profile does not prove policy authority legal validity or enforcement and detailed interoperability needs an ODRL profile.

normative_definition: Indicates whether the health dataset is public restricted or non-public.
intended_capability: Require exactly one access-tier classification and validate the tier-specific value.
known_limit: Three values are too coarse for detailed legal conditions and the draft assertion does not prove authorization.
source_pinpoint: HealthDCAT-AP Release 7 §Dataset.accessrights; tier-specific SHACL dct:accessRights constraints

normative_definition: Legislation mandating creation or management of the dataset.
intended_capability: Require at least one legislation link for each health dataset.
known_limit: Property semantics concern creation or management and cannot substitute for a reviewed legal-applicability assessment.
source_pinpoint: HealthDCAT-AP Release 7 §Dataset.applicablelegislation; SHACL Dataset applicableLegislation minimum count

normative_definition: The Article 51 health-data category to which the dataset belongs.
intended_capability: Require at least one health-sector category for catalogue discovery and scope review.
known_limit: Draft controlled vocabulary is hosted on an acceptance domain and category assignment remains a factual/legal judgment.

normative_definition: The health data access body responsible for providing access to the dataset.
intended_capability: Identify exactly one responsible HDAB and link it to required contact information.
known_limit: A metadata assertion does not establish legal designation competence or current responsibility.

normative_definition: Indicates whether the dataset contains structured data for which variables can be described in machine-readable form.
intended_capability: Trigger conditional variable-level metadata for structured datasets.
known_limit: The boolean does not itself describe structure or prove that a negative value is substantively correct.
source_pinpoint: HealthDCAT-AP Release 7 §Dataset.structureddata; SHACL hasStructuredData datatype and count constraints

normative_definition: Links the dataset to a CSVW TableGroup describing variables columns or fields.
intended_capability: Provide conditional machine-readable variable metadata for structured health data.
known_limit: CSVW structure does not by itself express clinical semantics quality or legal sensitivity of each variable.
source_pinpoint: HealthDCAT-AP Release 7 §Dataset.variables; conditional sh:or constraint for hasStructuredData and hasVariables

normative_definition: Identifies coding systems used in the health dataset.
intended_capability: Identify clinical or health coding systems used by dataset values.
known_limit: Optional and does not record version coverage local extensions or code-level correctness without additional metadata.

normative_definition: The number of records contained in the dataset.
intended_capability: Record a basic dataset-size measure.
known_limit: Optional count has no counting method snapshot time or integrity proof and is not an Article 78 quality metric by itself.
source_pinpoint: HealthDCAT-AP Release 7 §Dataset.numberOfRecords; SHACL datatype and max-count constraint

normative_definition: The number of unique individuals represented in the dataset.
intended_capability: Record the represented-person count for scope and coverage review.
known_limit: Optional count lacks identity-resolution method uncertainty snapshot time and disclosure-risk controls.

normative_definition: Describes populations or demographic groups covered by the dataset.
intended_capability: Describe represented population for discovery and representativeness assessment.
known_limit: Free text has no denominator sampling method uncertainty or controlled demographic semantics and cannot establish representative coverage.

normative_definition: The period for which the health data is retained.
intended_capability: Record a dataset-level retention interval.
known_limit: Does not state legal basis trigger deletion event exemptions or actual enforcement.

normative_definition: Links a dataset to associated quality annotations constrained by the draft profile to Quality Certificate resources.
intended_capability: Attach a quality certificate or annotation to a health dataset.
known_limit: No Article 78 dimension metric value method unit timestamp provenance or legal sufficiency is supplied by this link alone.
source_pinpoint: HealthDCAT-AP Release 7 §Dataset.qualityannotation; Release 7 range.ttl dqv:hasQualityAnnotation range constraint

normative_definition: A physical digital conceptual or other thing with fixed aspects; it may be real or imaginary.
intended_capability: Represent a versioned dataset distribution document model or evidence object in provenance.
known_limit: Entity typing does not prove identity integrity or evidential reliability.

normative_definition: Something occurring over time that acts upon or with entities including using transforming or generating them.
intended_capability: Represent collection cleaning annotation aggregation quality measurement or other data lifecycle events.
known_limit: Generic activity typing does not establish that a legally required operation occurred or was adequate.

normative_definition: Something bearing responsibility for an activity for an entity's existence or for another agent's activity.
intended_capability: Identify a person organization or software agent associated with evidence and lifecycle actions.
known_limit: A provenance agent is not automatically the legally responsible actor or statutory role-holder.

normative_definition: Relates an entity to an activity whose completion resulted in the entity.
intended_capability: Link a dataset version measurement report or metadata record to the activity that generated it.
known_limit: Does not prove the stated generation event or completeness of inputs.

normative_definition: Relates an activity to an entity used before during or at the activity.
intended_capability: Record source datasets files methods or plans used by a processing activity.
known_limit: Does not establish that all inputs were disclosed or lawfully used.

normative_definition: Relates a generated entity to another entity from which it was derived through an activity.
intended_capability: Represent dataset lineage and transformation dependencies.
known_limit: Does not identify the transformation method or determine whether derivation was legally permitted without additional activity and policy evidence.

normative_definition: Relates an entity to an agent to whom it is attributed.
intended_capability: Attribute a dataset metadata record or evidence artifact to an agent.
known_limit: Attribution is not equivalent to legal responsibility ownership authorship or approval.

normative_definition: Relates an activity to an agent bearing responsibility for it.
intended_capability: Associate lifecycle or measurement activity with the responsible executing agent.
known_limit: Association does not by itself encode the instrument-specific legal role authority or accountability allocation.

normative_definition: Relates an entity to a primary source on which it depends for its content.
intended_capability: Link extracted requirements or metadata evidence to an authoritative primary source.
known_limit: The relation does not establish authoritative status authenticity or faithful extraction.

normative_definition: Records the time at which an entity was completely created and became available for use.
intended_capability: Timestamp creation of a versioned dataset measurement or evidence artifact.
known_limit: A stated timestamp is not independently authenticated.

normative_definition: Links an activity to a qualified association that can identify agent role and plan.
intended_capability: Represent agent role and plan context for a processing or review activity.
known_limit: Qualified provenance roles remain technical/contextual and must not replace instrument-specific legal-role determinations.

normative_definition: Identifies the function of an entity or agent with respect to an activity in a qualified influence.
intended_capability: Attach a versioned operational role to a qualified provenance relation.
known_limit: Role values require a separate controlled profile and cannot infer legal status from organization type.

normative_definition: Relates an association to a plan intended by an agent to achieve goals in the associated activity.
intended_capability: Link a processing quality-assessment or review activity to its method or plan.
known_limit: Does not demonstrate execution fidelity adequacy or approval of the plan.

normative_definition: An observation evaluating a resource against a specific quality metric.
intended_capability: Represent a metric result for a dataset or distribution.
known_limit: The Note supplies no legal metric catalogue threshold or required measurement procedure.

normative_definition: A standard for measuring a quality dimension; an observation links to the metric through dqv:isMeasurementOf.
intended_capability: Define the measure against which a quality value is produced.
known_limit: No normative EHDS or AI Act metric definitions are supplied.

normative_definition: A quality characteristic relevant to a consumer in selecting whether a resource is fit for purpose.
intended_capability: Group metrics under characteristics such as completeness accuracy timeliness or consistency.
known_limit: Section 7 example dimensions are non-normative and fitness remains purpose-dependent.

normative_definition: A group of quality dimensions under a common broader topic.
intended_capability: Organize quality dimensions into a profile-specific taxonomy.
known_limit: No normative category hierarchy is mandated.

normative_definition: A Web Annotation whose body contains a quality assessment certificate or feedback about a resource.
intended_capability: Attach human or machine quality commentary to a dataset or distribution.
known_limit: An annotation records an assertion but does not establish accuracy authority or legal sufficiency.

normative_definition: A quality annotation that associates a resource with a quality certificate.
intended_capability: Represent a certificate attached to a dataset or distribution.
known_limit: No trust framework issuer competence certificate scheme validity or revocation semantics are supplied.

normative_definition: Relates a resource to a quality measurement performed for it.
intended_capability: Attach one or more explicit metric results to a dataset or distribution.
known_limit: DQV imposes no minimum count and the link does not ensure the legally relevant dimensions were measured.

normative_definition: Identifies the resource on which a quality measurement is computed.
intended_capability: Bind a measurement to the exact dataset distribution or other measured resource.
known_limit: Version identity and measurement scope still require explicit resource versioning.

normative_definition: Identifies the metric used for a quality measurement.
intended_capability: Link each value to an explicit metric definition.
known_limit: Metric existence does not establish method validity or legal relevance.

normative_definition: The value of a quality measurement.
intended_capability: Record the observed measurement result.
known_limit: Datatype unit uncertainty confidence and acceptance threshold must be supplied separately.

normative_definition: Identifies the unit associated with a quality measurement value.
intended_capability: Make numeric quality results interpretable and comparable.
known_limit: DQV does not mandate a unit vocabulary or dimensional-consistency checks.

normative_definition: Identifies the expected datatype for values produced by a metric.
intended_capability: Declare the expected representation of metric results for validation.
known_limit: Datatype validation alone does not check formula method or plausibility.

normative_definition: Relates a metric to the quality dimension it measures.
intended_capability: Classify a metric under a legally or operationally relevant quality dimension.
known_limit: The dimension and mapping require a separate reviewed profile.

normative_definition: A non-empty group of permissions prohibitions or duties expressing rules over assets.
intended_capability: Represent a machine-readable policy governing access or use of an asset.
known_limit: Policy syntax does not establish legal authority validity monitoring or enforcement.

normative_definition: A rule permitting an action on an asset and optionally requiring duties or constraints.
intended_capability: Represent allowed access or downstream use.
known_limit: Legal permission scope and enforceability depend on external law contract and authority.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.2 Rule Class and §2.3 Permission Class

normative_definition: A rule prohibiting an action on an asset subject to any stated constraints.
intended_capability: Represent prohibited access or use.
known_limit: Does not determine governing law legal exceptions conflict priority or enforcement.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.2 Rule Class and §2.4 Prohibition Class

normative_definition: A rule requiring an action that may serve as an obligation consequence or remedy in a policy.
intended_capability: Represent policy duties such as attribution anonymization deletion or compensation.
known_limit: An ODRL duty is a policy construct and not automatically a binding legal obligation.

normative_definition: An atomic condition comparing a left operand with a right operand using an operator.
intended_capability: Represent temporal purpose spatial recipient count or other policy conditions.
known_limit: Open-textured legal conditions require a versioned profile and reviewed interpretation.

normative_definition: An absolute IRI uniquely identifying a policy.
intended_capability: Give a policy a stable reference identifier.
known_limit: Uniqueness and persistence depend on issuer governance.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.1 Policy Class — uid relation

normative_definition: Relates a policy to a permission rule.
cardinality: 0..*; at least one of permission|prohibition|obligation per Policy
intended_capability: Attach permitted actions to a policy.
known_limit: Rule completeness and conflict resolution require processor and profile context.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.1 Policy Class — permission relation

normative_definition: Relates a policy to a prohibition rule.
cardinality: 0..*; at least one of permission|prohibition|obligation per Policy
intended_capability: Attach prohibited actions to a policy.
known_limit: Legal priority exceptions and remedies remain external unless explicitly profiled.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.1 Policy Class — prohibition relation

normative_definition: Relates a policy to a duty that must be fulfilled.
cardinality: 0..*; at least one of permission|prohibition|obligation per Policy
intended_capability: Attach standalone duties to a policy.
known_limit: Policy duty expression does not prove fulfilment or legal enforceability.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.1 Policy Class — obligation relation

normative_definition: Identifies the asset to which a rule applies.
cardinality: CONTEXT_DEPENDENT; explicit or inherited in compact policy representation
intended_capability: Bind permissions prohibitions and duties to a dataset distribution service or other asset.
known_limit: Asset identity and version must be externally governed.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.2 Rule Class — target relation

normative_definition: Identifies the action governed by a permission prohibition or duty.
intended_capability: Represent the type of access use transformation transfer or policy duty.
known_limit: Generic actions need profile semantics to align with instrument-specific legal conduct.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.2 Rule Class; ODRL Vocabulary 2.2 §3 Actions and §4 Common Vocabulary

normative_definition: Identifies the party assigning a rule.
intended_capability: Identify the policy-granting or duty-imposing party.
known_limit: Does not prove the party has authority to assign the rule.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.2 Rule Class — assigner relation

normative_definition: Identifies the party to whom a rule is assigned.
intended_capability: Identify the policy recipient or duty bearer.
known_limit: Does not determine the recipient's instrument-specific legal role or eligibility.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.2 Rule Class — assignee relation

normative_definition: Relates a rule to a condition that refines when its action is permitted prohibited or required.
intended_capability: Attach purpose time spatial recipient count or other conditions to a rule.
known_limit: Constraint interpretation and conflict handling require a shared ODRL profile.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.2 Rule Class — constraint relation

normative_definition: Identifies the property of an asset party action or environment being compared in an atomic constraint.
intended_capability: Select the policy fact constrained by a rule.
known_limit: A generic operand may not capture the legal definition or evidence needed to evaluate the condition.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.6 Constraint Class; ODRL Vocabulary 2.2 §4.5 Left Operands

normative_definition: Identifies the comparison operator used by an atomic constraint.
intended_capability: Express equality ordering inclusion or other comparison semantics.
known_limit: Operator semantics do not define data acquisition trust or legal interpretation of operands.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.6 Constraint Class; ODRL Vocabulary 2.2 §3.8 Operators

normative_definition: Supplies the value against which the left operand is compared in an atomic constraint.
intended_capability: Supply a purpose time recipient geography count or other constraint value.
known_limit: Datatype unit vocabulary and authoritative value source must be profiled.
source_pinpoint: ODRL Information Model 2.2 §2.6 Constraint Class — rightOperand relation

normative_definition: Relates an asset to a policy governing it.
intended_capability: Attach use and access policy to a dataset distribution or service asset.
known_limit: The relation records an asserted policy and not governing authority legal effect or technical enforcement.

normative_definition: A shape that validates focus nodes against node-level constraints.
intended_capability: Define constraints applying to dataset service policy measurement or evidence nodes.
known_limit: Validation establishes only conformance to the selected shape under recorded assumptions.

normative_definition: A shape with exactly one path that validates values reached from focus nodes along that path.
intended_capability: Define field-level cardinality datatype class value and logical constraints.
known_limit: Property-shape success does not verify factual correctness of values.

normative_definition: Targets as focus nodes the instances of a specified class under the selected entailment regime.
intended_capability: Select which resource types a shape validates.
known_limit: Target expansion can differ with entailment; the regime must be recorded.

normative_definition: Identifies the SHACL property path used to obtain value nodes from a focus node.
intended_capability: Bind a property shape to the metadata field or path it validates.
known_limit: Complex paths may reduce readability and do not define business semantics.

normative_definition: Requires at least the specified number of value nodes for each focus node.
intended_capability: Validate mandatory metadata and minimum multiplicity.
known_limit: A value's presence does not establish completeness substance or truth.

normative_definition: Requires no more than the specified number of value nodes for each focus node.
intended_capability: Validate maximum multiplicity and functional fields.
known_limit: Duplicate semantics and identity still depend on RDF term equality and profile design.

normative_definition: Requires each value node to be a SHACL instance of the specified class.
intended_capability: Validate object-resource class membership.
known_limit: Results depend on the chosen entailment regime and cannot establish real-world category truth.

normative_definition: Requires each value node to be a literal with the specified datatype.
intended_capability: Validate dates booleans numeric counts and metric-value datatypes.
known_limit: Correct datatype does not establish plausible value units method or factual accuracy.

normative_definition: Requires each value node to have the selected RDF node kind such as IRI literal or blank node.
intended_capability: Require persistent IRI references or literal values as appropriate.
known_limit: IRI syntax does not ensure resolution persistence or authority.

normative_definition: When true constrains a focus node to properties enumerated by its property shapes except explicitly ignored properties.
intended_capability: Detect unexpected metadata properties in tightly governed exchange profiles.
known_limit: Closed shapes can reject valid extensions and should not be used as a general legal completeness claim.

normative_definition: The result graph root describing whether the data graph conforms and containing validation results.
intended_capability: Record reproducible technical-conformance output.
known_limit: A report is specific to one data graph shapes graph entailment regime and processor execution.

normative_definition: Boolean on the validation report indicating whether validation produced no results with severity sh:Violation.
intended_capability: State the binary result of the selected SHACL validation run.
known_limit: Must never be presented as substantive legal compliance or factual truth.

normative_definition: A result node identifying a constraint failure and its focus node source and optional path value and message.
intended_capability: Record field-level validation evidence and actionable diagnostics.
known_limit: A result identifies graph non-conformance under the selected shape rather than legal non-compliance.
source_pinpoint: SHACL Recommendation §3.6 Validation Report — validation results

normative_definition: Relates a validation report to one of its validation results.
intended_capability: Enumerate constraint failures in a validation report.
known_limit: An empty result set is meaningful only with the matching shapes and run provenance.

normative_definition: Records the severity associated with the source shape of a validation result.
intended_capability: Classify validation findings for processing and review.
known_limit: Severity levels other than sh:Violation do not affect sh:conforms and do not encode legal materiality.
source_pinpoint: SHACL Recommendation §2.1.4 Severity and §3.6 Validation Report

normative_definition: Identifies the focus node that failed a constraint.
intended_capability: Trace a validation finding to the affected metadata resource.
known_limit: Does not identify the responsible human or real-world object without additional provenance.

normative_definition: Identifies the path associated with the value nodes that caused a validation result when applicable.
intended_capability: Trace a finding to the affected field or graph path.
known_limit: Not every constraint has a result path and paths do not provide legal issue classification.

normative_definition: Describes methods used during acquisition or processing to address identifiable bias in training data.
intended_capability: Document methods intended to address training-data bias.
known_limit: Optional free text records a claimed method but not bias examination results effectiveness residual bias representativeness or Article 10 compliance.

normative_definition: The date the data was originally collected as supplied by the uploader.
intended_capability: Require an original collection date for an ML dataset.
known_limit: A single date may not represent a collection interval refreshes or source-specific dates and uploader assertion is not independently verified.

normative_definition: Describes the method used to collect the data as supplied by the uploader.
intended_capability: Document how dataset observations or records were collected.
known_limit: Optional free text lacks structured sources sampling protocol uncertainty original purpose and independent verification.

normative_definition: Describes processing that transformed acquired data into training testing or validation data such as cleaning normalization or augmentation.
intended_capability: Document preparation and curation steps relevant to AI dataset governance.
known_limit: Free text does not structurally distinguish annotation labeling cleaning updating enrichment aggregation parameters agents timestamps or outputs.

normative_definition: A documented trail accounting for the origin of data.
intended_capability: Record a narrative account of dataset origin.
known_limit: Literal provenance is not structured lineage and does not ensure completeness authority or evidence integrity; PROV-O links remain necessary.

normative_definition: Describes methods used during acquisition or processing to detect unsuitable data sources in light of intended model uses.
intended_capability: Document screening methods for illegal inappropriate or otherwise unsuitable source data.
known_limit: Optional free text does not record screening results decisions remedies legal tests or ongoing monitoring.

normative_definition: Relates a distribution to an attribute or column that forms part of it.
intended_capability: Describe distribution-level features or columns used for training testing or validation.
known_limit: Optional feature declarations do not establish full schema coverage or semantic correctness.

normative_definition: An attribute or column associated with an ML dataset distribution and described by mandatory title and type properties.
intended_capability: Represent model-relevant dataset attributes as identifiable resources.
known_limit: The profile does not ensure every variable is declared or capture all statistical semantic privacy and quality characteristics.

normative_definition: A mandatory name for the feature.
intended_capability: Provide a human-readable feature identifier.
known_limit: A label does not define semantics datatype provenance or measurement scale.

normative_definition: A mandatory classification of the feature.
intended_capability: Classify a dataset feature for machine-learning use.
known_limit: The profile does not supply a complete legally relevant feature taxonomy or prove correct classification.

normative_definition: Relates each distribution to one or more quality measurements.
intended_capability: Require at least one quality measurement on each ML dataset distribution.
known_limit: The profile does not ensure coverage of legally relevant dimensions define measurement methods or establish substantive sufficiency.

normative_definition: The total count of individual data entries or observations in the distribution.
intended_capability: Record distribution size for quantity and coverage review.
known_limit: Optional count lacks a counting method snapshot time uncertainty or record-level evidence.

normative_definition: Relates a machine-learning model to each dataset used to train it.
intended_capability: Trace a model to mandatory training-dataset references.
known_limit: Does not identify exact distribution split version sampling run or whether the dataset was lawfully and suitably used.
source_pinpoint: MLDCAT-AP 3.1.0 §Machine Learning Model — trained on row (it6:trainedOn)

normative_definition: A mandatory classification for the quality represented by the measurement.
intended_capability: Classify a distribution quality result.
known_limit: This differs from canonical DQV's explicit dqv:isMeasurementOf Metric and inDimension pattern and may need a profile bridge.

normative_definition: A mandatory value for the quality measurement.
intended_capability: Require an observed value for each ML quality measurement.
known_limit: No datatype unit formula method uncertainty threshold provenance or evaluation time is mandatory in this profile row.

normative_definition: A Croissant description must type its top-level resource as schema.org Dataset and provide the required dataset-level fields.
intended_capability: Represent the top-level ML-ready dataset description.
known_limit: Schema-level typing does not establish dataset identity ownership source authority or legal scope.

normative_definition: A Croissant dataset must declare conformance to the versioned specification IRI.
intended_capability: Pin the dataset metadata record to Croissant 1.1.
known_limit: A declaration is not proof of conformance or legal compliance and multiple specification IRIs may be present.
source_pinpoint: Croissant 1.1 §Namespaces and §Dataset-level Information required properties — dct:conformsTo

normative_definition: A Croissant dataset requires one or more distribution values typed as cr:FileObject or cr:FileSet.
intended_capability: Enumerate files and file collections forming the ML dataset package.
known_limit: Declared resources may still be unavailable altered unauthorized or incompletely described.
source_pinpoint: Croissant 1.1 §Dataset-level Information — Modified and Added Properties; §Resources

normative_definition: The Croissant class for an individual dataset file and a subclass of schema:DataDownload.
intended_capability: Represent downloadable files with URL size format and optional checksum.
known_limit: File metadata does not prove content integrity unless the checksum is present verified and tied to the retrieved bytes.

normative_definition: A collection of homogeneous files in a container optionally selected by inclusion and exclusion patterns.
intended_capability: Represent archive or directory contents as dataset resources.
known_limit: Pattern evaluation describes package layout but not semantic completeness or legal admissibility.

normative_definition: A set of structured records obtained from one or more sources with structure expressed as fields.
intended_capability: Describe record-level structure across tabular nested and unstructured modalities.
known_limit: A RecordSet description need not prove full data coverage or accurate source extraction.

normative_definition: A component of a RecordSet representing a table column or nested data structure.
intended_capability: Represent variables features labels and nested elements with sources and datatypes.
known_limit: Field metadata does not establish statistical properties sensitivity or legal relevance.

normative_definition: Describes how data are extracted or transformed from files file sets or record sets to populate a field.
intended_capability: Represent machine-actionable source extraction and transformation paths.
known_limit: Does not supply responsible agent execution timestamp result integrity or legal authority without provenance extensions.

normative_definition: Relates a dataset description to structured RecordSet definitions.
intended_capability: Attach record and variable structure to the dataset.
known_limit: Optional relation means Croissant conformance does not necessarily provide record-level structure for every dataset.
source_pinpoint: Croissant 1.1 §Format Example and Appendix 1 context — cr:recordSet

normative_definition: Relates a RecordSet to data elements appearing in its records.
intended_capability: Enumerate columns variables or nested elements in a record structure.
known_limit: The specification's MANY cardinality does not itself establish full enumeration or a minimum.

normative_definition: Identifies a DataSource FileObject or FileSet from which field data originate.
intended_capability: Trace a field to its source file collection or extraction description.
known_limit: Optional relation does not guarantee complete lineage and source assertions need provenance evidence.
source_pinpoint: Croissant 1.1 §RecordSets — Field property table and DataSource introduction

normative_definition: Identifies an extraction method selecting content such as a column file property or JSON path from a source.
intended_capability: Describe deterministic selection of source data for a field.
known_limit: Does not record software version execution result or validation of the extraction.
source_pinpoint: Croissant 1.1 §RecordSets — DataSource property table and Extract subsection

normative_definition: Identifies one or more transformations applied to source data after extraction.
intended_capability: Represent preprocessing transformations in a machine-loadable dataset description.
known_limit: Transformation semantics are narrower than a full governed processing activity and lack agent time plan and evidence by themselves.
source_pinpoint: Croissant 1.1 §RecordSets — DataSource property table and Transform subsection

normative_definition: A SHA-256 checksum for FileObject content; strongly recommended for files in versioned datasets.
intended_capability: Support integrity checking and reproducible dataset versions.
known_limit: Optional checksum must be recomputed against retrieved bytes and does not prove source authority or legal permissibility.
source_pinpoint: Croissant 1.1 §Resources — FileObject property table; §Dataset Versioning/Checkpoints — Checksums

normative_definition: A semantic datatype used to identify arbitrary dataset partitions including training validation and test splits.
intended_capability: Identify training validation test or other dataset partitions.
known_limit: Does not record sampling rationale leakage controls representativeness or actual model use.

normative_definition: A semantic datatype identifying label data typically represented as fields in a RecordSet.
intended_capability: Mark fields or complex annotations as ML labels.
known_limit: Does not describe annotation workforce protocol disagreement quality review or label validity without additional properties.

normative_definition: A recommended dataset version using a major minor patch convention described by Croissant.
intended_capability: Identify dataset content versions for reproducibility and change analysis.
known_limit: Version semantics depend on producer discipline and do not ensure immutable retrieval.

normative_definition: Text describing the dataset's collection process.
intended_capability: Document the collection process for AI dataset review.
known_limit: Optional free text does not structure sources sampling roles original purpose or evidence and is not Article 10 compliance.

normative_definition: Text classifying the data collection method using recommended values such as surveys direct measurement software collection or web APIs.
controlled_vocabulary: Recommended non-closed list in RAI property table
intended_capability: Classify one or more collection modes.
known_limit: Recommended strings are not a formal controlled vocabulary and do not capture method parameters or legality.

normative_definition: Text describing missing data in structured or unstructured form.
intended_capability: Document known missing data for quality and gap review.
known_limit: No missingness metric mechanism pattern uncertainty threshold or remediation evidence is required.

normative_definition: Text describing the raw data or source from which the dataset was produced.
intended_capability: Document original source material.
known_limit: Free text is not a structured provenance chain and does not prove source authority ownership consent or licence.

normative_definition: Date-time values representing the start and end timeframe of collection.
intended_capability: Record temporal scope of the collection process.
known_limit: The MANY date-time structure does not formally distinguish start from end in the property table and does not model source-specific intervals.

normative_definition: Text describing the data-imputation process when applicable.
intended_capability: Document how missing values were imputed.
known_limit: Does not encode executable method parameters affected fields validation or bias effects.

normative_definition: Text describing data manipulation when applicable.
intended_capability: Document manipulation performed during dataset preparation.
known_limit: Broad free text does not distinguish cleaning updating enrichment aggregation or responsibility and execution evidence.

normative_definition: Text describing steps used to bring collected data into a form processable by an ML model or algorithm.
intended_capability: Document filtering cleaning and other preprocessing steps.
known_limit: Does not encode ordered executable transformations versions agents outputs or quality effects.

normative_definition: Text describing labels or ratings produced and how annotation tasks were authored or performed.
intended_capability: Document annotation and labeling design.
known_limit: Free text does not guarantee record-level linkage workforce consent quality review or inter-annotator analysis.

normative_definition: Text identifying platforms tools or libraries used to collect human annotations.
intended_capability: Identify tooling used in annotation and labeling.
known_limit: Does not require tool version configuration operator or audit evidence.

normative_definition: Text describing conversion of raw annotations into final labels including uncertainty disagreement and systematic group differences.
intended_capability: Document annotation aggregation disagreement and validation considerations.
known_limit: Does not require metrics methods values thresholds or record-level evidence.

normative_definition: Text describing updating timeframes maintainers versioning and deprecation policies.
intended_capability: Document dataset update maintenance and deprecation arrangements.
known_limit: Does not prove implementation ownership service levels or change notification.

normative_definition: Text describing sensitive human attributes present in a dataset.
controlled_vocabulary: Recommended attribute categories in RAI property table
intended_capability: Flag personal or sensitive information for governance review.
known_limit: Narrative disclosure does not determine GDPR or AI Act legal category necessity safeguards lawful basis or actual content.

normative_definition: Text describing biases identified in the dataset when applicable.
intended_capability: Document known bias for safety and fairness review.
known_limit: No examination method statistical result mitigation residual risk or representativeness test is required.

normative_definition: Text describing known generalization limits quality issues source limitations and non-recommended uses.
intended_capability: Document known shortcomings and limits of dataset use.
known_limit: Does not require remediation status severity evidence or a complete Article 10 gaps-and-remedies analysis.

normative_definition: Text describing recommended dataset uses such as training testing validation development production or fine tuning.
controlled_vocabulary: Recommended use labels in RAI property table
intended_capability: Document intended or recommended ML dataset uses.
known_limit: Does not model the AI system's geographic contextual behavioral and functional setting or legally determine suitable use.

normative_definition: A work in a legislative corpus including an enacted act or a component such as an article.
intended_capability: Identify the abstract legal act or provision referenced by metadata.
known_limit: Does not represent an extracted atomic rule modality applicability conditions or compliance result.

normative_definition: The intellectual realization of a legal resource as a sequence of signs differentiated for example by language or version.
intended_capability: Identify language- or version-specific legal text expressions.
known_limit: Expression identity choices may vary by publisher and do not establish legal status or faithful extraction.

normative_definition: A physical or electronic embodiment of a legal expression with a required eli:format value.
intended_capability: Identify PDF XML paper or other manifestations of a legal expression.
known_limit: Format does not prove authenticity official status content equivalence or source status.
source_pinpoint: ELI core 1.3 versioned OWL — owl:Class http://data.europa.eu/eli/ontology#Format

normative_definition: A component of a legal act at arbitrary precision such as chapter article paragraph or list item.
intended_capability: Identify stable article paragraph point or other provision subdivisions.
known_limit: By itself, the class identifies a structural subdivision but does not represent the complete traceability information used in this study, such as the extracted proposition, source version, registered dependencies, their interpreted effect, or applicability. ELI does not prohibit those additions, but they require other properties, components, or documented rules.

normative_definition: Relates a work to another work in which it is physically included such as an article within a text.
intended_capability: Link a legal subdivision to its containing act or higher subdivision.
known_limit: Physical inclusion must not be confused with conceptual or temporal membership and no closed-world parent cardinality is imposed.

normative_definition: The adoption or signature date of a work expressed as xsd:date.
intended_capability: Record adoption or signature date of the legal source.
known_limit: Does not distinguish adoption from signature where both matter and is not the publication entry-into-force or application date.

human_label: first date of entry into force
normative_definition: The first date on which any part of a legal resource or expression came into force.
intended_capability: Record first entry into force of a legal source.
known_limit: Does not represent staged application article-specific dates transition rules or later amendments.

normative_definition: The jurisdiction from which the legal resource originates.
controlled_vocabulary: EU Administrative Territorial Unit authority table recommended by ontology definition
intended_capability: Identify source jurisdiction using controlled territorial identifiers.
known_limit: Origin jurisdiction does not determine territorial applicability to a concrete use configuration.

normative_definition: Classifies the type of a legal resource such as regulation directive law or parliamentary act.
controlled_vocabulary: ELI ResourceType scheme; EU resource-type authority table recommended for EU resources
intended_capability: Classify the legal instrument type.
known_limit: Document type does not determine binding status applicability hierarchy or legal effect by itself.
